feedback: The model demonstrates strong overall performance on this malware analysis benchmark, achieving exact match accuracy of 72.2% (13/18 questions correct) with an average Jaccard similarity of 0.88. Parse quality is perfect with no parse failures. The model shows particular strength in process injection detection, ransomware behavior analysis, network IOC extraction, and credential access topics, achieving perfect scores on several of these. The model's primary weaknesses manifest as over-selection errors (adding extra incorrect options) and under-selection errors (missing one correct option), both occurring in roughly equal measure. Specific failure cases: Q2 (API Hooking) - over-selected by adding F (RegOpenKeyA) which is not in the gold set; Q4 (Defense Evasion for killers) - over-selected F (mutant creation) instead of G (timestomping T1070.006); Q8 (Network IOC for ransomware/WannaCry) - under-selected, missing A (kill-switch sinkhole domain contact); Q13 (Evasion Techniques for um_unhooking) - under-selected, missing H (high-entropy packed content); Q14 (Process Injection for um_unhooking) - under-selected, missing H (Thread Local Storage); Q16 (Process Injection Detection for um_unhooking) - significant error, predicted {A,C,D,G} vs gold {A,B,C,D,F}, missing B and F while adding G. Q17 (Behavioral Analysis for remcos) - major over-selection, predicted all 7 options vs gold {B,C,D,E,G}. The model tends slightly toward over-selection overall (avg predicted 4.11 vs avg gold 3.94). Attack family breakdown shows the model performs perfectly on ransomware (3/3) and near-perfectly on infostealers (4/5) and remcos (4/5 excluding the behavioral analysis question). The um_unhooking family is the weakest area with only 2/5 exact matches, and killers shows 2/3. Hard questions show reduced performance (1/2 exact match). Key training recommendations: (1) Focus on um_unhooking process injection questions, especially distinguishing Thread Local Storage from other injection sub-techniques; (2) Train on behavioral analysis questions requiring precise threat_level discrimination (malicious vs suspicious vs informative); (3) Improve evidence grounding for network behavior questions involving kill-switch sinkhole detection; (4) Reduce over-selection tendency by training the model to more carefully verify each candidate option against report evidence before including it.
strengths: Perfect parse rate: all 18 model answers were correctly formatted and parseable.
Strong performance on ransomware behavior analysis (3/3 exact matches), demonstrating solid understanding of anti-recovery techniques and process injection sub-techniques.
Excellent performance on process injection detection questions where API call evidence is clear (Q3, Q5, Q6, Q11, Q15 all correct).
Strong credential access topic performance (Q18 correct) showing good understanding of infostealer credential harvesting APIs and strings.
Reliable network IOC extraction for Remcos RAT (Q10 correct) and ransomware WannaCry network behavior (Q7 correct, partial miss on Q8).
Consistent exact match on evasion technique questions for infostealers (Q1 correct) and Remcos static analysis (Q12 correct).
Good calibration overall: average predicted count (4.11) is close to average gold count (3.94).

weaknesses.description: The model struggles with um_unhooking samples, achieving only 2/5 exact matches. Errors include missing Thread Local Storage injection (Q14), missing high-entropy packed content evasion (Q13), and a significant process injection detection error (Q16) where the model missed 'wrote 32 bytes to a remote process' (B) and 'svchost connects outbound after injection' (F) while incorrectly adding GetProcAddress (G).

Q17 (Remcos behavioral analysis) shows severe over-selection: the model selected all 7 options (A-G) while the gold set is only {B,C,D,E,G}. The model failed to distinguish malicious-rated signatures from suspicious/informative ones, including A (modifies auto-execute registry - suspicious) and F (sends traffic without HTTP header - suspicious) which are not rated malicious.

On hard evasion questions (Q1 and Q13), the model missed one correct option in Q13 (H: high-entropy packed content for um_unhooking). The model correctly identified A,C,D,E,G but missed H despite the report containing high-entropy section indicators mapped to T1027.009.

The killers family shows 2/3 exact matches. Q4 (Defense Evasion for Pykspa.Generic) had an error where the model selected F (named mutant creation) instead of G (timestomping T1070.006). The mutant is informative/not a defense evasion indicator in the MITRE mapping, while timestomping is explicitly mapped.

Q8 (WannaCry network behavior) missed option A (kill-switch sinkhole domain contact). The report clearly shows the malware contacts 'www.iuqerfsodp9ifjaposdfjhgosurijfaewrwergwea.com' which is the WannaCry kill-switch domain. The model identified C, D, F correctly but missed this well-known WannaCry behavioral indicator.

Q2 over-selected by including F (RegOpenKeyA@ADVAPI32.DLL) which appears as an informative string reference but was not listed as a hooked API in the hook detection signatures. The gold set only includes Process32NextW, FindFirstFileW, and send@WS2_32.DLL as hooked APIs per the hooks-0, hooks-2, and hooks-5 signatures.

Hard questions show reduced performance: 1/2 exact matches (Q1 correct, Q13 incorrect). The model misses subtle evidence indicators on hard questions that require comprehensive enumeration of all relevant report artifacts.
